findings.finding: Task 4's detection call git -C remote get-url origin requires unwrapped invocation but R19 lint flags any such call without # allow-direct; plan did not originally spell out the escape.
findings.evidence_quote: Detection: [ -d "$target/.git" ] = is-git-repo; git -C "$target" remote get-url origin = origin URL.

findings.finding: Task 6 originally declared 1-5 files but hermetic coverage needs 6-10 fixture files (stubs, fake-repo helpers, non-git dir, synthetic pyproject). Plan now acknowledges 6-10 range in Files field.
findings.evidence_quote: tests/test_install.sh, tests/fixtures/install/ (new directory with any fixture files needed)

findings.finding: Task 7's Files list was 'justfile' but Context directed editing tests/test_install.sh — P6 inconsistency. Now lists both.
findings.evidence_quote: Simpler: add it as a step inside tests/test_install.sh as the first test case

findings.finding: Task 6 claimed tests/test_hooks.sh convention covers stderr-ordering assertions; it does not. Test harness must add ordered-stderr capture plumbing.
findings.evidence_quote: Follow the shell-testing pattern from tests/test_hooks.sh (pass/fail counters, per-case pass/fail helpers, exit 0 if all pass).

findings.reviewer_angle: install.sh vs cortex upgrade safety divergence
findings.finding: README rewrite in Task 10 adds no guidance on when to use curl|sh re-run vs cortex upgrade. Users have divergent dirty-tree semantics on two paths they treat as interchangeable.
findings.evidence_quote: remove only the manual clone + uv tool install -e step since the bootstrap now covers them.

findings.reviewer_angle: install.sh vs cortex upgrade safety divergence
findings.finding: Task 4's 'Critical invariant: no rm -rf' framing is true but misleading — git pull --ff-only on a dirty tree with non-overlapping paths can still interleave upstream commits with uncommitted edits.
findings.evidence_quote: **Critical invariant**: no rm -rf anywhere in the file.

findings.reviewer_angle: install.sh vs cortex upgrade safety divergence
findings.finding: Task 6 test matrix originally omitted dirty-tree fixture for install.sh re-run. Not yet added; pending user decision on whether to add dirty-tree abort to install.sh.
findings.evidence_quote: R10 (idempotent re-run: two consecutive runs both exit 0; ~/.cortex/.git/HEAD unchanged; third run after synthetic [project.scripts] addition makes the new console script resolvable)

findings.finding: Task 11 originally ran install.sh once against the real GitHub URL, validating first-install only. Now updated to run twice for live idempotence coverage.
findings.evidence_quote: Task 11: run bash install.sh in a disposable HOME=$tmpdir sandbox pointing at the actual charleshall888/cortex-command GitHub URL

findings.finding: Overview originally omitted idempotence despite R10 being a named top-level requirement. Now added 'idempotent re-runs' to Overview feature list.
findings.evidence_quote: Overview: Ship install.sh at the repo root (POSIX sh, shellchecked, [cortex-install]-prefixed stderr logging, a run() wrapper enforcing exit-1 contract)
